reason: One brain-dead playstyle (spam a single common recipe) out-earned every intended playstyle by four orders of magnitude. The changes below lift discovery, quests, and industry into viability while trimming the spam runaway and its ingredient graveyard.
A_Sprinter printed 231k coins brewing nothing but Rootmoss potions at 97% utilization. The most-spammed common was over-valued; trimming it taxes single-recipe spam at the source.
Starter-node common that fed the spam economy and then rotted in inventory (44,048 unbrewed in the Sprinter run). Lowered to reduce the value sloshing through the opening loop.
Second-worst graveyard offender (26,411 unbrewed). Same rationale as Firepetal.

The Sprinter brews only Rootmoss, so 40% of every gather at the starter node (firepetal+dewcap) became a 70,459-unit ingredient graveyard. Concentrating the node on its primary output cut the Sprinter graveyard to 54,440.

entity: value_mult — mental attrs: mana / resonance / insight / radiance (configStore.formulas)
reason: B_Completionist discovered 80 unique potions but earned only 11,648 coins — discovery was economically pointless. These attributes appear almost exclusively on uncommon+ ingredients, so the buff rewards complexity without touching the Sprinter's attribute-poor commons.
Void is an end-tier attribute (Void Essence, Voidlily, Riftspore). It carried no payoff, so the 'esoteric but volatile' end-game ingredients had no reason to be brewed.

entity: value_mult — cosmic attrs: chrono / gravitas / entropy / soul / mutation (configStore.formulas)
reason: The legendary 'esoteric' tier (Chronopearl, Starmarrow, Entropyshard, God's Eye) existed but was worthless to brew. A measured buff (first tried at 0.03 — it sent the Completionist to 530k — then dialed back to 0.018) makes end-game potions a genuine payoff without a new runaway.

C_Industrialist hit a brick wall at machine #3 (250k was unreachable in 6h), stalling at 2 machines and 50% utilization. The old curve jumped ×50 then ×40; the new curve is a smooth ~×20 per tier so building out the factory is actually attainable.

D_QuestHunter completed 0.6 quests in 6h and ended with 13.6 coins. Quests are meant to be 'massive payouts' but barely beat selling the same potions. Steeper bonuses make fulfilling a quest clearly worth the workforce reshuffle.

Quota = score / avg-recipe-complexity, so the old bands demanded brewing 30-100+ of one specific potion name — impossible at gather-limited throughput. Roughly halving the bands made quests completable (D went 0.6 → 5.5 completions).

Requiring up to 5 different potion names per quest forced the player to maintain many recipes at once — unfulfillable for a gather-limited brewer. Fewer names per quest makes each one a focused, achievable objective.
